I found some information about Bastardiastrum cinctum. 

Bastardiastrum cinctum is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to South America. Bastardiastrum cinctum belongs to the genus Bastardiastrum. This plant is classified within the tribe Barnadesieae. The classification of Bastardiastrum cinctum is further detailed in various botanical resources.

The plant's leaves and stems have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. intro: Bastardiastrum cinctum, also known as Bastardiastrum, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. Traditionally, Bastardiastrum cinctum has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including digestive issues, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bastardiastrum cinctum include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Bastardiastrum cinctum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with colds and flu.
